title: acrostichum appendiculatum var. hamiltonianum
intro: Acrostichum appendiculatum var. hamiltonianum is a variety of the Acrostichum appendiculatum fern species, commonly known as the fan-leaved or water fern, native to tropical and subtropical regions. This plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Acrostichum appendiculatum var. hamiltonianum include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being applied topically to the skin in the form of poultices or ointments.
